mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity risk premiums compress and forward equity returns are driven by earnings growth expectations rather than discount rate changes. Sustained near-zero rates predict positive 20-day returns as liquidity-driven rallies dominate.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window in zero-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment decline (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings. Negative payroll momentum predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity markets front-run earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances following PAYEMS declines > 0.5%, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly measure consumer demand strength. When both series decline simultaneously, aggregate demand is weakening, signaling lower corporate revenues and equity valuations. Joint deterioration in these demand proxies predicts negative 20-day SPX returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 344380.0 million, HOUST at 583.0 thousand as of 2009-02-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% simultaneously, or the joint signal has lower predictive power than either series alone (Rank IC difference < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic capacity utilization. Sustained INDPRO decline below 98.0 indicates supply-side weakness and deflationary pressure. Under deflationary regimes, real equity returns compress despite nominal price stability, predicting negative 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO < 98.0 and declining for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO < 98.0 and declining, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation expectations (proxied by CPIAUCSL momentum) interact with monetary policy stance (FEDFUNDS). When inflation is stable or declining while rates are near zero, real rates are deeply negative, incentivizing equity allocation and predicting positive 20-day returns. The mechanism requires both conditions simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 212.714 and FEDFUNDS at 0.18% as of 2009-03-15
regime_hint: zero rates with stable or declining inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances when both FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and CPIAUCSL momentum < 0.2%, or the interaction term adds no explanatory power beyond individual series (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 8.5% signals labor market stress that persists for quarters. Elevated unemployment predicts sustained earnings pressure and negative equity returns over 60-day horizons as markets price in prolonged demand weakness and wage deflation.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 8.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 24-month rolling window in high-unemployment regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) lead consumer spending and employment by 2-3 months. A sharp HOUST decline signals future demand weakness. This always-on trend mechanism predicts negative 5-day SPX returns when HOUST falls, as forward-looking equity markets immediately discount future earnings deterioration.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST momentum and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10), or SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances following HOUST declines.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure real economic momentum. When both decline simultaneously, the economy is in synchronized contraction. This interaction-driven signal predicts negative 20-day SPX returns as markets price in recession risk and earnings collapse.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1% month-over-month AND PAYEMS declines > 0.3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.3755 and PAYEMS at 133019.0 as of 2009-03-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline simultaneously, or the joint signal has lower predictive power than either series alone (Rank IC difference < 0.01).
